name: Hy3
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts hybrid fast/slow-thinking model, 295B total / 21B active
note: Full release of Tencent's Hunyuan 3 (upgraded from an April 2026 preview build), Tencent's first genuinely unrestricted Apache 2.0 flagship. Newer than Hunyuan-A13B already in corpus (dated 2025-06-27). Pricing converted from RMB 1/RMB 4 per million input/output tokens at ~7.1 RMB/USD.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, from Tencent's own Hy3-preview model card (295B-A21B); full July release benchmark table not separately located [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing 87.2 figure was pulled from the April-2026 Hy3-preview build's GitHub model card (explicitly flagged in the prior verificationNotes as a stand-in because 'the full July release benchmark table' hadn't been located). That table has now been located: the official tencent/Hy3 model card's benchmark-appendix image reports GPQA Diamond = 90.4 for the actual shipped Hy3 (295B-A21B) model, v]
SWE-bench Verified; also confirmed via HF's structured Evaluation-results widget (SWE-bench/SWE-bench_Verified = 78) and the model card's benchmark-appendix image
Confirmed via both HF's Evaluation-results widget (ScaleAI/SWE-bench_Pro = 57.9) and the benchmark-appendix image
Terminal-Bench 2.1, from the model card's benchmark-appendix image (Agentic Coding section)
BrowseComp, from the benchmark-appendix image (Agentic Search section)

HLE, no-tools/text-only variant, listed under the appendix's 'Reasoning' task category (a separate 'HLE with tools' row under 'STEM Agent' scores 53.2, but that is a distinct tool-augmented setup, not standard HLE)

USAMO 2026, from Tencent's official Hy3 model-card benchmark table (STEM/Reasoning section). Not previously tracked for this model. Independently corroborated verbatim (72.0) by a second outlet, valueaddvc.com ('Tencent's Hy3 Beats GLM-5.2 at Half the Size'), both citing the same official figure.

verificationNotes:
Benchmark numbers (MMLU 87.42, MMLU-Pro 65.76, GPQA Diamond 87.2, LiveCodeBench-v6 34.86) come from the preview build's GitHub model card (Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview), which Tencent itself published -- treated as self-reported since it is the vendor's own repo, and may differ slightly from the July full-release numbers which were not separately published with a benchmark table in sources checked.

2026-07-16: Resolved the six missing-core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) for the full July-2026 Hy3 release by downloading and directly inspecting the official model card's two benchmark images (huggingface.co/tencent/Hy3, assets/benchmark.png and assets/benchmark-appendix.png). The appendix table is comprehensive (6 task categories: Agentic Coding, Agentic Search, Working Agent, STEM Agent, Reasoning, Context Learning; ~30 named benchmarks with Hy3-preview vs. Hy3 columns) and deliberately does not include MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU anywhere -- Tencent replaced the legacy academic set with newer evals (USAMO 2026, MathArena Apex, ArxivMath, HorizonMath, IMOAnswerBench, PHYBench, CMT-Benchmark, SuperChem for math/reasoning; SWE-bench Multilingual/Verified/Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, NL2repo, DeepSWE for coding) for the shipped model's official comparison. Cross-referenced the April-2026 predecessor checkpoint's own GitHub README (Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview, raw README fetched directly): it does report MMLU 87.42 (5-shot), MMLU-Redux 86.86 (5-shot), MMLU-Pro 65.76 (5-shot), MATH 76.28 (4-shot), GSM8K 95.37 (4-shot), and LiveCodeBench-v6 34.86 (1-shot) -- but these describe a distinct predecessor checkpoint, not the shipped Hy3, and per this corpus's known variant-conflation issue (see dataset-audit-2026-07) are not carried over to this entry, consistent with how this same file's GPQA/SWE-bench figures were previously superseded rather than reused once real full-release numbers were located. Neither the preview nor the full-release official materials publish AIME or HumanEval under any label or alias (the preview substitutes MBPP-plus and CRUXEval-I for code, not HumanEval). MMMU was not pursued as a scored benchmark because Hy3 is a text-only model in this corpus's own spec (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false) -- MMMU requires image inputs and is architecturally inapplicable. Independent-leaderboard check: Epoch AI's benchmarking hub returns no Hy3 page (404), Vals.ai's models listing does not track any Tencent model as of this check, and Artificial Analysis's Hy3 page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/hy3) shows only an aggregate Intelligence Index (41) and speed/price figures, no per-benchmark MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/MMMU scores. All six gaps are recorded as omitted_no_evidence; this reflects a genuine, deliberate absence in Tencent's own published materials for the shipped model, not a sourcing failure.
